source: Sed, Awk & Grep - Sed command: How sed works, its options, and addressing lines

questions.q: Which memory area holds the line sed is currently processing?
options: The pattern space (with an optional hold space)
questions.why: sed reads a line into the pattern space, processes it, outputs, and clears it; the hold space is extra storage.

options: Suppress automatic printing (so only commands like p produce output)
questions.why: -n plus the p command prints only the lines you select.

questions.q: Which option enables extended regular expressions in sed?
What does the address '23,26p' select, and what does $ mean?
options: Lines 23 to 26 (an addr1,addr2 range); $ is the last line
The 23rd to 26th character; $ is a variable
questions.why: addr1,addr2 is a line range; $ matches the last line; /regexp/ and first~step are other address forms.
